ingredients.name: Ikebukuro Eki Higashiguchi (Bus)
ingredients.address: Ikebukuro Eki Higashiguchi, 1-chōme-28 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 3, Marin and Gojo meet at Ikebukuro East Exit to begin their first cosplay shopping trip together, kicking off their fabric and wig hunt across Ikebukuro.

ingredients.name: Hotel 0 (Zero)
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-28-7 Dogenzaka, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 11, this Dogenzaka love hotel serves as the photoshoot location for Marin's Shizuku-tan succubus cosplay, leading to one of the most memorable and awkward moments between Marin and Gojo.

ingredients.name: Hair & Makeup Breeze
ingredients.localName: ヘアメイク ブリーズ
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒339-0057 Saitama, Iwatsuki Ward, Honcho, 3 Chome−1−1
ingredients.entityContext: Located in Iwatsuki, this hair and makeup salon in Episode 6 is referenced in the context of the doll town district where Gojo grew up, reflecting the local businesses that surround his family's workshop.

ingredients.name: Sunshine City
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-1-2 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-8630, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 5, Marin and Gojo attend their very first cosplay event held at Sunshine City in Ikebukuro, modeled after the real Acosta cosplay conventions held on the complex's rooftop, where Marin debuts her Shizuku-tan cosplay.

ingredients.name: Haguregumo
ingredients.address: 浮浪雲 池袋クラウンハイツ 3 Chome-8-3 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0022
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 3, Marin and Gojo stop at this Ikebukuro izakaya-style restaurant during their cosplay shopping outing, one of several local spots they visit together in the neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Hachiko Statue
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 10, Marin takes Gojo on a Shibuya shopping date and they pass through the iconic Hachiko meeting spot outside Shibuya Station, a recognizable landmark that anchors their day out in the city.

ingredients.name: Hachigatsu no Kujira
ingredients.address: 28-13 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Udagawacho bar in Shibuya appears in Episode 11 as part of the backdrop for the succubus cosplay arc scenes set in the Shibuya nightlife area.

ingredients.name: East Ikebukuro Central Park
ingredients.localName: 豊島区立東池袋中央公園
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-1-6 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This park just steps from Sunshine City appears in Episode 5 as part of the Ikebukuro cosplay event setting, serving as the outdoor public space surrounding the venue where fans of the series gather for real cosplay meetups.

ingredients.name: Chichibu Shrine
ingredients.address: 1-3 Banbamachi, Chichibu, Saitama 368-0041, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 12, Wakana and Marin meet at a shrine for a summer festival, and Chichibu Shrine serves as the real-world model for this emotional season finale scene where Gojo sees Marin in her yukata and their bond deepens.

ingredients.name: Chakottoomiyaten
ingredients.address: エクセレント大宮ビル 1F, 1 Chome-9-4 Sakuragicho, Omiya Ward, Saitama, 330-0854, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Chacott's Omiya store, a professional dance and stage supplies shop, appears in Episode 8 as one of the specialist stores the characters visit to source cosplay accessories and performance materials.

ingredients.name: Burger King Shibuya Center Street
ingredients.localName: バーガーキング 渋谷センター街店
ingredients.type: ファースト フード店
categories: ファースト フード店
ingredients.address: Center Bldg., 1F, 25-5 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 10, Marin and Gojo pass through Shibuya Center Street during their casual summer date, with this Burger King visible as one of the street-level landmarks faithfully recreated in the anime's Shibuya scenes.

ingredients.name: Bangkok Mookataw
ingredients.localName: タイ料理 バンコクムーカタウ
ingredients.address: 16１F, 1 Chome-15-15 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Thai restaurant in Minamiikebukuro appears in Episode 3 as one of the spots Marin and Gojo visit during their Ikebukuro cosplay shopping day, reflecting the area's lively food scene near the costume stores.

ingredients.name: Swallowtail Ikebukuro
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒170-0013 Tokyo, Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 3 Chome−12−5 加藤第一ビル 4Ｆ
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 3, Marin drags Gojo to the Swallowtail wig specialty shop in Ikebukuro to pick out the perfect wig for her Shizuku-tan cosplay, one of the most charming shopping scenes in the early episodes.

ingredients.name: Tenkaippin - Shibuya
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1-11 Ebisuminami, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 10, Marin takes Gojo to this famous ramen chain in Shibuya during their summer date, where they eat together in a scene that highlights how naturally their relationship has grown beyond cosplay.

ingredients.name: Tower Records Shibuya
ingredients.localName: タワーレコード 渋谷店
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-22-14 Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0041, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tower Records Shibuya appears in Episode 11 as part of the Shibuya streetscape during the succubus cosplay arc, its distinctive building faithfully recreated as Marin and Gojo navigate the neighborhood for their photoshoot.

ingredients.name: Yuzawaya WACCA Ikebukuro Store
ingredients.address: WACCA池袋 3F, 1 Chome-8-1 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 3, Marin takes Gojo to Yuzawaya WACCA Ikebukuro, a large craft and fabric store, to shop for cosplay materials. Fans confirmed the store sign and interior were recreated almost exactly in the anime, making it a must-visit pilgrimage stop.

ingredients.name: animate Ikebukuro main store
ingredients.localName: アニメイト 池袋本店
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-20-7 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Animate Ikebukuro, Japan's largest anime merchandise chain store, is featured in the opening sequence of My Dress-Up Darling, establishing Ikebukuro as the heartland of otaku and cosplay culture in the series.

ingredients.name: Skechers Shibuya store
ingredients.localName: スケッチャーズ 渋谷
ingredients.address: Shu Shibuya, 1F, 25-9 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Skechers store on Shibuya Center Street appears in Episode 10 as part of the Udagawacho streetscape during Marin and Gojo's summer date, with the anime accurately reproducing the storefront as the pair walk through the area.

ingredients.name: Shim-Misato Station
ingredients.address: 2-chōme-4 Shinmisatorara City, Misato, Saitama 341-0009, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shin-Misato Station serves as the meeting point in Episode 9 where the group gathers to visit the nearby IKEA for cosplay prop and fabric shopping, an outing that reveals Shinju's desire to also experience cosplay.

ingredients.name: Shibuya Center-Gai Street
ingredients.address: Udagawachō, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shibuya Center-gai, the iconic pedestrian shopping street, is depicted in Episode 7 as a backdrop for the characters' Shibuya outings, capturing the busy youth culture and street fashion that mirrors Marin's own spirited personality.

ingredients.name: Seibu Realty Solutions
ingredients.address: 14階, 1 Chome-16-15 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0022, Japan

ingredients.name: Naka-ikebukuro Park
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-16-1 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Naka-Ikebukuro Park appears in the opening sequence of the series, used to establish the Ikebukuro neighborhood atmosphere as the cosplay and anime hub where much of the show's action takes place.

ingredients.name: Mitsuinoripaku Minamiikebukuro 1-chome
ingredients.localName: 三井のリパーク 南池袋１丁目 駐車場
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-7-14 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0022, Japan

ingredients.name: Masudaya
ingredients.address: ラ・メール垂水 2F, 3-18 Kandacho, Tarumi-ku, Kobe, Hyogo 655-0027, Japan

ingredients.name: Maison Creperie
ingredients.localName: メゾン クレープリー渋谷宇田川町店
ingredients.address: Kn Shibuya Bldg. I, 13-11 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This crepe shop in Udagawacho, Shibuya, appears in Episode 10 during Marin and Gojo's summer date as one of the casual food stops that makes the day feel like a genuine first date between the two leads.

ingredients.name: Koji Ningyo
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒339-0066 Saitama, Iwatsuki Ward, Atagocho, 10−3
ingredients.entityContext: This real doll manufacturing workshop in Iwatsuki Ward is one of the inspirations for the Gojo family's hina doll shop, the heart of Wakana's world. Visiting Iwatsuki's doll craftsmen, including shops like this, informed the manga author's authentic portrayal of traditional ningyo craft.

ingredients.name: Kintaro Iwatsuki store
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒339-0057 Saitama, Iwatsuki Ward, Honcho, 3 Chome−1−20
ingredients.entityContext: This okonomiyaki restaurant in Iwatsuki's main shopping street appears in Episode 6 as a local eatery in the doll town neighborhood where Gojo grew up, grounding the show's portrayal of everyday life in Iwatsuki.

ingredients.name: Katase Higashihama Beach
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-15-1 Katasekaigan, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0035, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Katase Higashihama Beach near Enoshima is the real-world location for the spontaneous beach scene in Episode 8, where Marin invites Gojo on a spur-of-the-moment trip and he photographs her in the golden backlighting, a turning point in their relationship.

ingredients.name: Kashiwanoha Dental Clinic
ingredients.localName: 柏の葉デンタルクリニック
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒277-0872 Chiba, Kashiwa, Toyofuta, 409-100

ingredients.name: Iwatsuki Station
ingredients.address: 1-chōme-1 Honchō, Iwatsuki Ward, Saitama, 339-0057, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Iwatsuki Station is the gateway to the doll town district where Wakana Gojo grew up and his grandfather's shop is located. Episode 6 features this station as characters travel to and from the Iwatsuki neighborhood that defines Gojo's identity as a ningyo craftsman.

ingredients.name: Atelier Cache Cache
ingredients.localName: アトリエカシュカシュ
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒982-0807 Miyagi, Sendai, Wakabayashi Ward, Kokucho, 22-1
ingredients.entityContext: This art cafe in Iwatsuki appears in Episode 6 as one of the local spots in the doll town district, reflecting the creative and artisan culture of the neighborhood that shaped Wakana's dedication to traditional craftsmanship.

ingredients.name: Atago Shrine
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-5-3 Atago, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Atago Shrine in Iwatsuki Ward appears in Episode 1 as a neighborhood shrine near Gojo's family doll shop, establishing the traditional, craft-focused atmosphere of the town where Wakana was raised by his grandfather.
